name: Materials: Metals and Non-Metals
subject: chemistry
summary: Metals shine, conduct, and malleate. Non-metals are dull insulators. Watch their reactions with oxygen, water, and acids.

crushed.title: Metals = shiny conductors
crushed.text: Lustrous, malleable, ductile, sonorous. Good heat & electricity conductors.

crushed.title: Non-metals = opposite
crushed.text: Dull, brittle (as solids), poor conductors. Exception: graphite conducts electricity.

crushed.title: Metal + O₂ → Metal oxide
crushed.text: Most metals form basic oxides on burning. Na/K stored in kerosene to prevent this.

crushed.title: Metal + Acid → Salt + H₂
crushed.text: Zinc + HCl → ZnCl₂ + H₂↑. Hydrogen pops with a lit splint.

crushed.title: Displacement reaction
crushed.text: More reactive metal displaces less reactive one from its salt solution. Zn displaces Cu from CuSO₄.

items.label: Hardness
items.detail: Metals: generally hard. Non-metals: soft (S) to hard (diamond)

items.label: Malleability
items.detail: Metals: can be beaten into sheets. Non-metals: brittle

items.label: Conductivity
items.detail: Metals: conduct heat & electricity. Non-metals: insulators (except graphite)

items.label: Sonority
items.detail: Metals: ringing sound when struck. Non-metals: no

demos.title: Zinc + Hydrochloric Acid
demos.description: Zinc reacts with dilute HCl to produce hydrogen gas — the pop test confirms it.

steps.label: Add zinc
steps.description: Zinc granules dropped into dilute HCl in a test tube

steps.label: Bubbles form
steps.description: Effervescence — H₂ gas bubbles rise rapidly

steps.label: Pop test
steps.description: Lit splint at mouth gives a characteristic 'pop' sound

steps.label: Salt solution
steps.description: Zinc chloride solution remains in the test tube

demos.title: Magnesium + Oxygen
demos.description: Magnesium burns in air to form basic magnesium oxide.

steps.label: Heat Mg ribbon
steps.description: Magnesium held in flame with tongs

steps.label: Bright white flame
steps.description: Mg reacts vigorously with atmospheric O₂

steps.label: Basic oxide
steps.description: MgO dissolves in water to form Mg(OH)₂ — turns red litmus blue

demos.title: Zinc Displaces Copper
demos.description: Zinc (more reactive) kicks copper out of copper sulphate solution — blue colour fades.

steps.label: Blue solution
steps.description: Blue CuSO₄ solution in a beaker

steps.label: Add zinc strip
steps.description: Zinc strip dipped into blue solution

steps.label: Colour fades
steps.description: Blue colour slowly disappears as Cu²⁺ is displaced

steps.label: Red-brown deposit
steps.description: Copper metal deposits on the zinc strip surface

demos.title: Iron Rusting
demos.description: Iron reacts with oxygen and moisture to form rust — a slow oxidation.

steps.label: Moist air
steps.description: Iron exposed to both oxygen and water vapour

steps.label: Red-brown rust
steps.description: Flaky red-brown coating forms on iron surface

flows.title: Using Displacement to Compare Reactivity
flows.description: If metal A displaces metal B from its salt, A is more reactive than B.

steps.label: Setup
steps.description: Prepare copper sulphate (blue) solution in a beaker.

steps.label: Add metal
steps.description: Zinc is higher in reactivity series than copper.

steps.label: Reaction
steps.description: Zinc takes sulphate, copper deposits as solid.

steps.label: Observe
steps.description: Blue colour fades; red-brown copper coats the zinc.

steps.label: Conclusion
steps.description: More reactive metal always displaces less reactive one.

keyTerms.definition: Ability of metals to be beaten into thin sheets
keyTerms.analogy: Flattening dough with a rolling pin

keyTerms.definition: Ability of metals to be drawn into wires
keyTerms.analogy: Stretching chewing gum into a long string

keyTerms.definition: More reactive element replaces less reactive one in a compound
keyTerms.analogy: Stronger player takes the weaker one's spot on the team

keyTerms.definition: Oxide that reacts with both acids and bases (e.g. Al₂O₃)
keyTerms.analogy: A diplomat who talks to both sides

chapterSummary.overview: Elements are broadly classified as metals or non-metals based on physical and chemical properties. This chapter explores lustre, conductivity, malleability, reactions with oxygen, water, and acids, displacement reactions, and everyday uses of metals and non-metals.
keyPoints: Metals: lustrous, malleable, ductile, good conductors of heat and electricity.
Non-metals: dull, brittle, poor conductors (except graphite), form acidic oxides.
Metal + oxygen → metal oxide; reactive metals react with water and dilute acids producing H₂.
Displacement reaction: more reactive metal displaces less reactive metal from its salt solution.
Uses: iron (construction), aluminium (foil), copper (wires), sulphur and nitrogen in industry.
chapterSummary.examFocus: Compare metal vs non-metal properties. Write reaction of zinc with HCl. Explain displacement with an example.
